emotionalMoments.moment: Arrival in new country - confusion and fear
Language barriers - not understanding 'double-double' or 'feeling blue'
Working two jobs - overnight health care aide and daytime Tim Hortons
Frostbite scars - didn't know how to protect from cold
Visiting Sudan refugee camp - tiny room, 50°C heat, no water, no electricity
Sandstorm epiphany - wishing for Canadian winter, realizing perspective
Father jailed in Syria - Kurdish oppression
Arrival in US after 9/11 - strange time to be Muslim
Airport moment - man showing dog pictures, feeling American
Washing dishes - hands wrinkled like prunes, no gloves, no one told him
6-mile trek to work - watching Emory students, dreaming of college
Serendipity moment - brother's classmate's father is heart surgeon
Training one block from dishwashing restaurant - full circle
Childhood in Civil War - rockets hitting city, running from school
Dream crushed - can't be doctor, no education for refugees
Building life over 20 years - job, home, safety, status
August 15, 2021 - lost everything overnight, 'I was just somebody afraid of everything'
10 minutes to pack 20 years - backpack moment
Kabul airport hell - 8.5 hours outside under hail of bullets
Trash bag moment - refusing to leave without daughters, taking clothes back
Arriving with nothing - 'no job, no relations, no friends, no family, no home, no country, nothing'
Grieving multiple losses at once - house, work, people, while supporting daughters

struggle: Arrival in new country with nothing
perseverance: Working two jobs to support family

source1: I actually wished for what I hated most. Winter! I longed for the snow of Canada
When you are a refugee, you are a nobody. You've lost your identity
You won't always feel like a refugee
source2: The restaurant where I used to wash dishes is one block away from where I train as a cardiology fellow today
If I, as a refugee, could come here and become a physician 15 years later... this is the American Dream
I wished then that I had a mentor to motivate me
source3: I had 10 minutes, maybe 15 minutes, to put 20 years of my life in one backpack
I went back, took my clothes back from the trash bag and put them back on
I became a homeless person without anything: no job, no relations, no friends, no family, no home, no country, nothing
